entries.title: Commentary on the Catholic Epistles on second-peter 1:14
entries.excerpt: Knowing that the putting off of my tabernacle is very near. Most beautifully does blessed Peter call his demise not death, but the putting off of his tabernacle, because indeed for the perfect servants of God it is as if they cast off the bonds of the flesh, as travelers having completed their journey go to their own home for habitation instead of their tabernacle, as those deployed in expedition return to their fatherland after the enemy has been driven away or defeated. For they acknowledge only their own home, only their municipality, only their fatherland in the heavens. Of which also the apostle Paul says: "We know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens" (2 Cor. 5).

entries.title: INTRODUCTORY COMMENTARY ON 2 PETER on second-peter 1:14
entries.excerpt: The Lord Jesus showed Peter that his end was near either by a revelation through the Holy Spirit or by his response to Peter when he said: "Get behind me, Satan" or perhaps by dreams and visions in the night.

entries.title: Commentary on 2 Peter on second-peter 1:14
entries.excerpt: And in explanation of the reason for the constant reminder, he added that he knows that the dissolution of his body will soon follow.
